issues: StatsCard divs have onClick but no tabIndex, role, or onKeyDown (WCAG 2.1.1)
Todo input field has focus:outline-none with no alternative focus indicator (WCAG 2.4.7)

tests.name: duplicate-ids
tests.description: Check for duplicate ID attributes (WCAG 4.1.1 Parsing)

stat-card.explanation: StatsCard component hardcodes id="stat-card" but is rendered 3 times (stat-total, stat-completed, stat-pending), creating 3 elements with the same ID

tests.name: keyboard-accessible-click-handlers
tests.description: Elements with click handlers must be keyboard accessible (WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard)

details.explanation: div with onClick handler but no tabIndex, role='button', or onKeyDown. Keyboard users cannot activate this element.

tests.name: button-accessible-names
tests.description: Icon buttons must have accessible names (WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value)

details.explanation: Button contains only an SVG icon with no aria-label, aria-labelledby, or visible text. Screen readers will announce it as an unnamed button.
Button contains only an SVG icon with no aria-label, aria-labelledby, or visible text.

tests.name: image-alt-text
tests.description: Images must have alt text (WCAG 1.1.1 Non-text Content)

details.explanation: Image element lacks alt attribute entirely. Screen readers cannot describe this image.

tests.name: focus-visibility
tests.description: Focus indicators must be visible for all interactive elements (WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible)

elementsWithoutFocusIndicator.explanation: The input has class 'focus:outline-none' which removes the default focus outline, and no alternative focus indicator (such as box-shadow or border-color change) is applied via CSS. The 'focus:border-blue-500' class may provide a subtle indicator via border color, but the outline suppression is still problematic.

tests.name: tab-order
tests.description: Tab order should follow a logical reading sequence (WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order)

details.note: Tab order follows DOM order (nav -> breadcrumb -> main content). StatsCard elements with onClick are NOT in the tab order because they lack tabIndex, which is the keyboard accessibility bug.

recommendations.fix: Use the testId prop or a unique identifier as the id, e.g., id={testId} instead of id="stat-card"

recommendations.issue: StatsCard: div with onClick not keyboard accessible
recommendations.fix: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and an onKeyDown handler that triggers handleClick on Enter/Space
Add aria-label to btn-refresh (e.g., aria-label="새로고침") and btn-settings (e.g., aria-label="설정")
Add alt attribute to promo-banner.png image, e.g., alt="프로모션 배너" or alt="" if decorative
Replace focus:outline-none with a visible focus indicator, e.g., focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500
